---
title: Templates
description: Reference solution-architecture templates showing how the NAISH Hub powers AI use cases across public service delivery, government, health, agriculture, and education.
---

Templates are **reference solution architectures**. Each shows how a real class
of solution is put together, and – most importantly – **where the NAISH Hub fits
in and what AI it powers**. They are starting points a team can adapt: keep the
shape, swap in your own models, data, channels, and platforms.

The examples below are illustrative, not prescriptive. A template names a *kind*
of component – a records system, a messaging channel, an identity check – not a
specific product. Use whichever tools and platforms suit your project; the Hub is
the constant.

## What every template shows

- **The channels** users arrive through (chat, voice, apps).
- **The AI layer the Hub powers** – models served on Hub GPUs, an agent that
  reasons over a goal and calls tools (its *skills*), and retrieval that keeps
  answers grounded.
- **The supporting services and data** the solution reads and writes.
- **The external dependencies** it relies on (identity, payments, messaging,
  model sources).

The Hub's role is consistent across all of them: it is the **sovereign AI core**
– serving and fine-tuning models on national infrastructure – that these
solutions plug into. See
[GPU / Compute Support](/docs/capabilities/gpu-compute-support).
